subject: nltk.pathsec security policy
old: nltk.pathsec security policy was opt-in or not enforced by default
new: nltk.pathsec security policy is enforced by default
truth: Stop relying on the old lenient security defaults; the stricter nltk.pathsec security policy is now enforced by default, which may block previously allowed resource loads.
quote: Enforce the stricter `nltk.pathsec` security policy by default

from_fact: nltk.pathsec security policy was opt-in or not enforced by default
nltk.pathsec security policy is enforced by default

subject: nltk.data resource loading
old: nltk.data allowed protocol-less refs with path traversal and arbitrary reads
new: nltk.data blocks path traversal and arbitrary reads for protocol-less refs
truth: Stop using protocol-less references that traverse paths in nltk.data; they are now blocked to prevent path traversal and arbitrary reads.
quote: Block path traversal/arbitrary reads in nltk.data for protocol-less refs (#3467)

from_fact: nltk.data allowed protocol-less refs with path traversal and arbitrary reads
nltk.data blocks path traversal and arbitrary reads for protocol-less refs

subject: corpus readers and FS pointers
old: corpus readers and FS pointers allowed path traversal and absolute paths
new: corpus readers and FS pointers block path traversal and absolute paths
truth: Stop using path traversal or absolute paths in corpus readers and FS pointers; they are now blocked.
quote: Block path traversal/abs paths in corpus readers and FS pointers (#3479, #3480)

from_fact: corpus readers and fs pointers allowed path traversal and absolute paths
corpus readers and fs pointers block path traversal and absolute paths

subject: ZIP extraction in nltk.downloader
old: ZIP extraction in nltk.downloader was vulnerable to path traversal (CVE-2025-14009)
new: ZIP extraction in nltk.downloader is now secured against path traversal
truth: Stop relying on the old insecure ZIP extraction in nltk.downloader; it now blocks path traversal attacks during extraction.
quote: Fix CVE-2025-14009: secure ZIP extraction in nltk.downloader (#3468)

from_fact: zip extraction in nltk.downloader was vulnerable to path traversal (cve-2025-14009)
zip extraction in nltk.downloader is now secured against path traversal

subject: JSONTaggedDecoder
truth: Stop passing deeply nested JSON to JSONTaggedDecoder; unbounded recursion is now blocked (GHSA-rf74-v2fm-23pw).
quote: Fix GHSA-rf74-v2fm-23pw: unbounded recursion in JSONTaggedDecoder

subject: nltk.downloader checksums
truth: Stop relying on MD5 checksums in nltk.downloader; the built index now uses SHA256 checksums for downloads.
quote: Update download checksums to use SHA256 in built index

subject: StanfordSegmenter JAR validation
new: StanfordSegmenter JARs are validated using SHA256
truth: Stop using unverified StanfordSegmenter JARs; external StanfordSegmenter JARs are now validated using SHA256.
quote: Validate external StanfordSegmenter JARs using SHA256 (#3477)

from_fact: stanfordsegmenter jars are validated using sha256

subject: filestring() sandbox enforcement
truth: Stop assuming filestring() has no restrictions; optional sandbox enforcement has been added for filestring().
quote: Add optional sandbox enforcement for filestring() (#3485)

subject: resource loading hardening
old: resource loading was vulnerable to path traversal and SSRF/DNS-rebinding
new: resource loading is hardened against path traversal and SSRF/DNS-rebinding
truth: Stop relying on the old permissive resource loading; it is now hardened against path traversal and SSRF/DNS-rebinding attacks.
quote: Harden resource loading against path traversal and SSRF/DNS-rebinding

from_fact: resource loading was vulnerable to path traversal and ssrf/dns-rebinding
resource loading is hardened against path traversal and ssrf/dns-rebinding

subject: downloader path handling and XML entity expansion
old: downloader path handling was insecure and allowed XML entity expansion
new: downloader path handling is hardened and XML entity expansion is blocked
truth: Stop relying on the old insecure downloader path handling; XML entity expansion is now blocked.
quote: Harden downloader path handling and block XML entity expansion

from_fact: downloader path handling was insecure and allowed xml entity expansion
downloader path handling is hardened and xml entity expansion is blocked

subject: unsafe exec() usage in utility CLI
new: unsafe exec() usage in utility CLI has been replaced
truth: Stop relying on exec() behavior in the utility CLI; unsafe exec() usage has been replaced.
quote: Replace unsafe exec() usage in the utility CLI

from_fact: unsafe exec() usage in utility cli has been replaced

subject: unpickling user-provided pickles
old: unpickling user-provided pickles had no warning
truth: Stop unpickling user-provided pickles without caution; a warning is now emitted when unpickling user-provided pickles.
quote: Warn on unpickling user-provided pickles

from_fact: unpickling user-provided pickles had no warning

subject: pickled models (punkt, chunker, taggers)
truth: Stop using pickled models for punkt, chunker, and taggers; use the new pickle-free '_tab' packages instead.
quote: Replace pickled models (punkt, chunker, taggers) by new pickle-free "_tab" packages

subject: Wordnet synsets and relations ordering
old: Wordnet synsets and relations were sorted
new: Wordnet synsets and relations are no longer sorted
truth: Stop relying on Wordnet synsets and relations being sorted; sort in your own calling function when required.
quote: No longer sort Wordnet synsets and relations (sort in calling function when required)

from_fact: wordnet synsets and relations were sorted
wordnet synsets and relations are no longer sorted

subject: Wordnet Morphy suffix stripping
new: Wordnet Morphy only strips the last suffix
truth: Wordnet Morphy now only strips the last suffix, which restricts synsets() results compared to before.
quote: Only strip the last suffix in Wordnet Morphy, thus restricting synsets() results

from_fact: wordnet morphy only strips the last suffix

subject: CVE-2024-39705 security fix
truth: The fix for security vulnerability CVE-2024-39705 introduces a breaking change that may break existing code.
quote: Fix security vulnerability CVE-2024-39705 (breaking change)

subject: Python 3.6 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.6; NLTK 3.7+ requires Python 3.7 or newer.
quote: Drop support for Python 3.6, support Python 3.10 (#2920)

subject: omw corpus name
truth: Stop using 'omw' as the corpus name; use 'omw-1.4' instead.
quote: Rename omw to omw-1.4 (#2907)

subject: Tree get and set node methods
truth: Stop using Tree.get_node() and Tree.set_node() methods; they are deprecated.
quote: Deprecate Tree get and set node methods (#2900)

subject: return_str parameter in NLTKWordTokenizer and TreebankWordTokenizer
truth: Stop using the 'return_str' parameter in NLTKWordTokenizer and TreebankWordTokenizer; it is deprecated.
quote: Deprecate 'return_str' parameter in NLTKWordTokenizer and TreebankWordTokenizer (#2883)

subject: METEOR evaluation
truth: METEOR evaluation now requires pre-tokenized input instead of raw text.
quote: METEOR evaluation now requires pre-tokenized input

subject: nltk.usage() function
truth: Stop using nltk.usage(obj); use Python's built-in help(obj) instead.
quote: deprecate `nltk.usage(obj)` in favor of `help(obj)`

subject: Python 3.5 support
truth: NLTK 3.6.3 dropped support for Python 3.5; you must use Python 3.6 or later.
quote: Dropped support for Python 3.5

subject: edit_distance function
truth: edit_distance now computes Damerau-Levenshtein distance (including transpositions) instead of plain Levenshtein distance.
quote: edit_distance now computes Damerau-Levenshtein edit-distance

subject: Python 2 support
truth: Stop using NLTK with Python 2; NLTK 3.5+ requires Python 3.
quote: drop support for Python 2

subject: Tokenizer class
truth: Stop using the Treebank reference tokeniser; use NLTK's own Tokenizer class instead.
quote: create NLTK's own Tokenizer class distinct from the Treebank reference tokeniser

subject: FreqDist <= and >= operators
old: <= and >= were total orders (likely full comparators)
truth: Stop relying on <= and >= on FreqDist to be total/comparable for all pairs; they now implement partial orders so some comparisons may raise or be undefined.
quote: change implementation <= and >= for FreqDist so they are partial orders

from_fact: <= and >= were total orders (likely full comparators)

subject: Python 3.4 support
truth: Stop using NLTK with Python 3.4; NLTK 3.4.1+ drops Python 3.4 support.
quote: drop support for Python 3.4

subject: moses.py
truth: Stop using moses.py; it has been dropped due to an incompatible license.
quote: Drop moses.py (incompatible license)

subject: FrameNet corpus reader
old: fes(), lus(), exemplars() methods without frame parameter
new: fes(), lus(), exemplars() methods now require a frame parameter
truth: The fes(), lus(), and exemplars() methods on the FrameNet corpus reader now require a 'frame' parameter that was previously optional or absent.
quote: rewrite FrameNet corpus reader (adds frame parameter to fes(), lus(), exemplars() see https://www.nltk.org/howto/framenet.html)

from_fact: fes(), lus(), exemplars() methods without frame parameter
fes(), lus(), exemplars() methods now require a frame parameter

subject: Python 2.6 support
truth: Stop using Python 2.6; NLTK no longer supports it.
quote: Drop support for Python 2.6

subject: regexp tokenizer capturing groups
new: no capturing groups in regexp tokenizer
truth: Stop using capturing groups in regexp tokenizer; they are no longer supported.
quote: drop support for capturing groups in regexp tokenizer

from_fact: no capturing groups in regexp tokenizer

subject: default POS tagger
truth: The default POS tagger used by nltk.pos_tag is now the perceptron tagger instead of the previous default.
quote: Adopt perceptron tagger for new default POS tagger nltk.pos_tag

subject: evaluation metric interfaces argument order
truth: Evaluation metric interfaces now put reference before hypothesis; stop passing hypothesis before reference.
quote: consistent evaluation metric interfaces, putting reference before hypothesis

subject: POSTagger
truth: Stop using POSTagger, use StanfordPOSTagger instead.
quote: POSTagger renamed StanfordPOSTagger, NERTagger renamed StanfordNERTagger

subject: NERTagger
truth: Stop using NERTagger, use StanfordNERTagger instead.
quote: POSTagger renamed StanfordPOSTagger, NERTagger renamed StanfordNERTagger

subject: parse_ methods
truth: Stop using parse_ methods, use read_ or load_ methods instead.
quote: renamed remaining parse_ methods to read_ or load_, cf issue #656

subject: Tree initialization
truth: Stop passing a string to Tree() constructor; use Tree.fromstring() instead.
quote: Changed Tree initialization to use fromstring

subject: Tree.node
truth: Stop using Tree.node as an attribute; use Tree.label() to get the label and Tree.set_label() to set it.
quote: Tree.node -> Tree.label() / Tree.set_label()

subject: Chunk parser parameters
truth: Stop using top_node and chunk_node parameters in chunk parsers; use root_label and chunk_label instead.
quote: Chunk parser: top_node -> root_label; chunk_node -> chunk_label

subject: WordNet Synset properties
truth: Stop accessing WordNet Synset properties as attributes (e.g. Synset.definition); call them as methods (e.g. Synset.definition()).
quote: WordNet properties are now access methods, e.g. Synset.definition -> Synset.definition()

subject: relextract functions
truth: Stop using show_raw_rtuple() and show_clause() in relextract; use rtuple() and clause() instead.
quote: relextract: show_raw_rtuple() -> rtuple(), show_clause() -> clause()

subject: simplify_tags parameter
truth: Stop using simplify_tags=True; use tagset='universal' instead.
quote: replaced simplify_tags with support for universal tagset (simplify_tags=True -> tagset='universal')

subject: Punkt tokenizer default behaviour
old: Punkt did not realign sentence boundaries after trailing parenthesis and quotes
new: Punkt now realigns sentence boundaries after trailing parenthesis and quotes by default
truth: Punkt tokenizer now realigns sentence boundaries after trailing parenthesis and quotes by default, which may change sentence splitting output.
quote: Punkt default behavior changed to realign sentence boundaries after trailing parenthesis and quotes

from_fact: punkt did not realign sentence boundaries after trailing parenthesis and quotes
punkt now realigns sentence boundaries after trailing parenthesis and quotes by default

subject: classify.svm module
truth: Stop using nltk.classify.svm; use scikit-learn instead.
quote: deprecated classify.svm (use scikit-learn instead)

subject: ViterbiParser.nbest_parse method
truth: Stop using ViterbiParser.nbest_parse() and use ViterbiParser.parse() instead.
quote: changed ViterbiParser.nbest_parse to parse

subject: deprecated functions and associated warnings
truth: Deprecated functions have been removed entirely, so code relying on them will raise AttributeError or ImportError.
quote: removal of deprecated functions and associated warnings

subject: Mace parameter
truth: Replace max_models with end_size when using Mace to prevent hanging.
quote: Replaced max_models with end_size to prevent Mace from hanging

subject: WordNet morphy return value
new: returns the shortest returned form consistently
truth: WordNet's morphy now returns the shortest returned form instead of an arbitrary one, which may change which lemma is returned.
quote: Fixed WordNet's morphy to be consistent with the original implementation, taking the shortest returned form instead of an arbitrary one (issues 427, 487)

from_fact: returns the shortest returned form consistently

subject: WordNet similarity metrics return value on failure
truth: WordNet similarity metrics now return None instead of -1 when they fail to find a path.
quote: WordNet similarity metrics return None instead of -1 when they fail to find a path (Steve Bethard)

subject: FreqDist.pop() and FreqDist.popitems() cache invalidation
truth: FreqDist.pop() and FreqDist.popitems() now invalidate internal caches, which may change performance or behavior of subsequent cache-dependent operations.
quote: FreqDist.pop() and FreqDist.popitems() now invalidate the caches (issue 511)

subject: WordNet count return value
old: returned None when no count present
truth: WordNet now returns 0 instead of None when no count is present for a lemma.
quote: Return 0, not None, when no count is present for a lemma in WordNet

from_fact: returned none when no count present

subject: CoNLL corpus file naming
truth: Stop using CoNLL 'cat' files; they have been renamed to 'esp' (different language).
quote: Renamed CoNLL "cat" files to "esp" (different language)

subject: NLTK-Contrib distribution
old: NLTK-Contrib was part of the main NLTK distribution
new: NLTK-Contrib moved out of the main NLTK distribution into a separate distribution
truth: NLTK-Contrib is no longer part of the main NLTK distribution; it has been moved into a separate distribution.
quote: moved out of the main NLTK distribution into a separate distribution

from_fact: nltk-contrib was part of the main nltk distribution
nltk-contrib moved out of the main nltk distribution into a separate distribution

subject: nltk.foo.Bar naming convention
new: Names now available as nltk.Bar (some names modified)
truth: Stop using nltk.foo.Bar for significant functionality; use nltk.Bar instead (old names produce a deprecation warning).
quote: Names of the form nltk.foo.Bar now available as nltk.Bar for significant functionality; in some cases the name was modified (using old names will produce a deprecation warning)

from_fact: names now available as nltk.bar (some names modified)

subject: WordNet package
new: new WordNet corpus reader (contributed by Steven Bethard)
truth: Stop using the old pywordnet-based WordNet package; it has been moved to nltk_contrib and replaced by the new WordNet corpus reader.
quote: Moved old pywordnet-based WordNet package to nltk_contrib

from_fact: new wordnet corpus reader (contributed by steven bethard)

subject: cfg.py module
truth: Stop importing from nltk.cfg; import from nltk.grammar instead, which now incorporates dependency grammars.
quote: moved nltk/cfg.py to nltk/grammar.py and incorporated dependency grammars

subject: FreqDist.keys() and list(FreqDist)
old: FreqDist.keys() and list(FreqDist) returned keys in arbitrary/insertion order
new: FreqDist.keys() and list(FreqDist) return keys reverse-sorted by frequency value
truth: FreqDist.keys() and list(FreqDist) now return keys reverse-sorted by value (highest frequency first), not in insertion order.
quote: FreqDist.keys() and list(FreqDist) provide keys reverse-sorted by value, to avoid the confusion caused by FreqDist.sorted()

from_fact: freqdist.keys() and list(freqdist) returned keys in arbitrary/insertion order
freqdist.keys() and list(freqdist) return keys reverse-sorted by frequency value

subject: deprecated code from 0.8.* versions
truth: All deprecated code remaining from version 0.8.* has been removed; any code relying on those APIs will break.
quote: Removed deprecated code remaining from 0.8.* versions

subject: Brown Corpus section names
old: Brown Corpus sections had single-letter names (e.g. 'a')
new: Brown Corpus sections now have meaningful names (e.g. 'a' is now 'news')
truth: Brown Corpus section identifiers changed from single-letter codes to meaningful names; code referencing 'a' must now use 'news'.
quote: Brown Corpus sections now have meaningful names (e.g. 'a' is now 'news')

from_fact: brown corpus sections had single-letter names (e.g. 'a')
brown corpus sections now have meaningful names (e.g. 'a' is now 'news')

subject: Tree construction from strings
truth: Replace Tree.parse(s) with Tree(s) to construct trees from strings.
quote: trees can be constructed from strings with Tree(s) (cf Tree.parse(s))

subject: Corpus storage format
truth: Each corpus is now stored as a zip file instead of uncompressed directories, changing how data files are accessed.
quote: stored each corpus as a zip file for quicker installation and access, and to solve a problem with the Propbank corpus including a file with an illegal name for MSWindows (con.xml).

subject: Data directory name
truth: The data directory was renamed to nltk_data to facilitate installation, which may break hardcoded paths.
quote: renamed to nltk_data to facilitate installation

subject: Book chapter filenames
old: Book chapter filenames had a different naming scheme
new: Book chapter filenames changed to chNN format
truth: Book chapter filenames changed to chNN format, breaking any scripts or references using the old filenames.
quote: changed filenames to chNN format

from_fact: book chapter filenames had a different naming scheme
book chapter filenames changed to chnn format

subject: ConllChunkCorpusReader
new: bio_sents() and bio_words() methods added; ConllChunkCorpusView modified to support reading (word, tag, chunk_typ) tuples
truth: ConllChunkCorpusView now returns (word, tag, chunk_typ) tuples instead of previous format, breaking code that expects the old tuple structure.
quote: added bio_sents() and bio_words() methods to ConllChunkCorpusReader in conll.py to allow reading (word, tag, chunk_typ) tuples off of CoNLL-2000 corpus. Also modified ConllChunkCorpusView to support these changes.

from_fact: bio_sents() and bio_words() methods added; conllchunkcorpusview modified to support reading (word, tag, chunk_typ) tuples

subject: Punkt sentence segmenter
truth: The Punkt sentence segmenter was rewritten, which may change segmentation behavior for existing code relying on the old implementation.
quote: rewrite of Punkt sentence segmenter (Joel Nothman)

subject: Reuters corpus reader
new: bugfix for too many open files
truth: The Reuters corpus reader was fixed to avoid opening too many files, which changes its internal file-handling behavior and may affect code that relied on the old behavior.
quote: bugfix in Reuters corpus reader that causes Python to complain about too many open files

from_fact: bugfix for too many open files

subject: nltk.chunk.Regexp
truth: Stop using nltk.chunk.Regexp and use nltk.RegexpParser instead.
quote: nltk.chunk.Regexp -> nltk.RegexpParser

subject: nltk.tokenize.Line
truth: Stop using nltk.tokenize.Line and use nltk.LineTokenizer instead.
quote: nltk.tokenize.Line -> nltk.LineTokenizer

subject: nltk.stem.Porter
truth: Stop using nltk.stem.Porter and use nltk.PorterStemmer instead.
quote: nltk.stem.Porter -> nltk.PorterStemmer

subject: nltk.parse.ShiftReduce
truth: Stop using nltk.parse.ShiftReduce and use nltk.ShiftReduceParser instead.
quote: nltk.parse.ShiftReduce -> nltk.ShiftReduceParser

subject: StemI interface
truth: Stop using the class name StemI and use StemmerI instead.
quote: StemI -> StemmerI

subject: ParseI interface
truth: Stop using the class name ParseI and use ParserI instead.
quote: ParseI -> ParserI

subject: ChunkParseI interface
truth: Stop using the class name ChunkParseI and use ChunkParserI instead.
quote: ChunkParseI -> ChunkParserI

subject: ClassifyI interface
truth: Stop using the class name ClassifyI and use ClassifierI instead.
quote: ClassifyI -> ClassifierI

subject: ngram tagger and lookup tagger
old: separate lookup tagger and unigram tagger
new: lookup tagger collapsed into unigram tagger
truth: The lookup tagger has been collapsed into the unigram tagger; code relying on a separate lookup tagger class will break.
quote: rewritten ngram tagger code, collapsed lookup tagger with unigram tagger

from_fact: separate lookup tagger and unigram tagger
lookup tagger collapsed into unigram tagger

subject: corpus reader identification
old: using 'items' to identify parts of a corpus
truth: Corpus readers now use filenames instead of 'items' to identify parts of a corpus; code using the 'items' attribute will break.
quote: Minor redesign of corpus readers, to use filenames instead of "items" to identify parts of a corpus

from_fact: using 'items' to identify parts of a corpus

subject: numpy dependency
truth: The numpy dependency has been removed; code relying on numpy being available through NLTK will break.
quote: remove numpy dependency

subject: corpora package
truth: Stop using the old 'corpora' package; use the new 'corpus' package instead.
quote: new corpus package, obsoleting old corpora package

subject: nltk/contrib package
truth: Stop importing from nltk.contrib; import from nltk_contrib instead.
quote: moved nltk/contrib to separate top-level package nltk_contrib

subject: wordpunct tokenizer regex
truth: The wordpunct tokenizer now uses \w instead of a-zA-Z0-9, which changes tokenization behavior for non-English text and may produce different tokens for English text containing underscores or digits.
quote: changed wordpunct tokenizer to use \w instead of a-zA-Z0-9 as this will be more robust for languages other than English, with implications for many corpus readers that use it

subject: FreqDist
truth: Stop using FreqDist.count(sample); use FreqDist[sample] instead, because FreqDist now inherits from dict.
quote: FreqDist now inherits from dict, fd.count(sample) becomes fd[sample]

associative: how many times does word appear

subject: beta classify package
old: beta classify package in main NLTK
truth: Stop importing from the beta classify package in main NLTK; it has been moved to contrib.
quote: moved beta classify package from main NLTK to contrib

from_fact: beta classify package in main nltk

subject: classeq function
truth: Stop using classeq and use isinstance instead for type checking.
quote: replaced classeq with isinstance, for efficiency improvement, as suggested by Martin Ranang

subject: NLTK dependency
truth: NLTK-Lite no longer depends on the old NLTK package; code relying on NLTK being available as a dependency will break.
quote: removed NLTK dependency
